change: Added process.batchable + jobOperationBatch schema (migration, types) and wired the batchable flag end-to-end through the resources process form/table/validator with a red→green test
reason: slice complete; remaining: Build the batch-operations edge function (create/add/remove/update/dissolve/complete with the eligibility gate + proportional time-slicing), the get_batchable_operations RPC + get_active_job_operations_by_location re-declaration, production.models/service wrappers, the ERP batch planning board (dnd-kit + material facets), and the MES batch card + Complete Batch form
task: 1/1: Job Operation Batching — combine shared preparation across concurrent production orders
unverified: Only slice t1.s1 is implemented: the process.batchable flag. Verified working in the running app — created a process, toggled Batchable ON, saved, and the value persisted (Batchable column renders 'checked' in the processes table). However, NONE of acceptance criteria [0]-[9] can be reproduced because the surfaces they require do not exist in this worktree: no batch planning board route (grep of apps/erp/app/routes finds no batch planning board), no batch-operations edge function (absent from supabase/functions/), no MES batch card/Complete Batch form, no server validation/time-split/completion logic. AC[0]'s own behavior ('operations appear on the batch planning board') is unreachable since there is no board. This is absence-of-proof, not disproof — the batching behavior is not yet built rather than broken. To verify the feature, a human needs the remaining slices (board + edge function + MES completion) implemented, then can drag operations into batches, filter by material facets, and complete a batch.
